other_observers: David Waltman; Nathan Pieplow and Walter Szeliga are the original observers. Also observed by Christian Nunes, John Vanderpoel, Todd Deininger, Tom Wilberding, etc.
specific_location: Strip of land separating pond 5 from pond 4 in NE corner of Sawhill Ponds Open Space
description: This was a small bird bigger than a kinglet/goldfinch or most warblers/vireos; about the size of a housefinch. It had a fairly long and pointy black fill generally brownish gray on the back with the tail being darker. The chest was streak with difuse gray/brown streaks and the entire belly and vent was a bright, "house finch" pink.Behaviors: Flycatching from low branches down to the ground.Call: NAPlumage: adult female
